calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Machado for and with Christine Wu.
Carroll Collins Guardian Ad Litem in Propria Persona.

Mr. Collins stated that he has been doing family law for over forty years, that he has a strong feeling this is community property, that there is nothing to account and that costs of doing an accounting is of no benefit to the estate. Mr. Collins also stated that he endeavored to lower his fees in doing this and that he has incurred $1,900.00 in fees in 2015 and 2016. The First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-29-2017 Continued To Dec-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Off Calendar

Spousal Property Petition
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Machado & Ed Watson for and with Christine Wu. Carroll J. Collins III, Guardian ad Litem.

The Court asked Mr. Machado if he had the examiner's notes. Mr. Machado stated that he did and would respond, that regarding #3 their declaration explained the settlement proceeds, that the assets were acquired during marriage with community funds, cited Family Law Code 760 regarding assets accumulated during marriage and that they have set forth the source of the funds. Mr. Machado addressed 4a stating that it is an ancillary issue, that they disagree with the need to file a Petition for Final Distribution, that the settlement agreement included Ms. Wu individually and as administrator as a recipient of proceeds, that Ms. Wu was a party to the settlement agreement does not transmute her interests and that she is entitled to bring this petition. Mr. Machado addressed #1 regarding the Inventory & Appraisal, gave background information regarding getting Ms. Wu appointed, that their reasons for not doing an Inventory & Appraisal are that they did not want to prejudice the litigation and that the actual titling did not lend itself well to the litigation and that when the settlement agreement was approved the order directed an inventory of assets. Mr. Watson stated that when the probate was started they had concerns regarding the difficulty of valuing the entities and regarding things titled in other names being treated for tax purposes, that there were several corporations and an LLC, that valuation would be difficult, that they would have to help the Probate Referee, that they were concerned as to how the valuations would affect taxes and chose not to inventory the assets. Mr. Machado stated that the remaining item, the Schwab Account, is not disputed that it was opened prior to marriage, that some of it could be separate property, that the total account is only $6000.00, that it is not Ms. Wu's wish to parse out what may not be community property, that the objective is to accomplish the Court's goals, that they need advice on the best means to get there, that the most efficient is to deem it community property, that a 13100 is not applicable here and that there have been no claims. The Court stated that its concern is the interest of the children. Mr. Machado asked if the children have a right to the account what is an expedient way to deal with it. The Court stated that it is unclear as to what is community versus separate property, that it is too complicated to deal with under this petition, that the Court wants a corrected Inventory & Appraisal, an increased bond - via an ex parte application and a Petition for Final Distribution. Mr. Watson cited P.C. 28 & Family Law Code 760 that the property is presumed to be community property with no rebuttal, that it is definitive of the nature of this property, that if they do a Petition for Final Distribution is would be the same argument and would incur more cost. The Court stated that it is not satisfied that counsels analysis of community and separate property is accurate. Mr. Watson stated that it was all accumulated during marriage and that nothing is separate except for the Schwab account. The Court stated that some property is stated to be held as separate. Mr. Watson stated that it was for convenience. The Court stated again that counsel will need to file a corrected Inventory & Appraisal, increase bond and file a Petition for Final Distribution. The Court denied the Spousal Property Petition.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Spousal Property Petition

Off Calendar Jan-30-2013 Continued To Apr-03-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-10-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Julia Chan and Edward Watson for and with Christine Wu.

The Court informed Counsel that it was not inclined to waive bond. Counsel explained that the assets are illiquid, that they are business assets (property in San Francisco), that Ms. Wu has no access to the business assets, that the business Partners are not cooperative and that they do not know the value of the assets or rental value because the business Partners will not provide Ms. Wu with any information. In addition, Counsel stated that Ms. Wu would not be able to sell the properties, that the Decedent had a fractional interest and that a 1/3 ownership is held in the Decedent's name. Counsel also explained that Ms. Wu. needs to be appointed so that she has authority to obtain information on the assets, that the Decedent was receiving a salary, suggested a minimum bond until they can obtain more information and spoke of changing the petition to request limited authority. The Court suggested that Counsel could do research on their own to determine rental values and property values. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with limited authority which will not include authority to undertake any transactions with regard to the real property (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). Bond is set at $25,000.00. A status hearing with regard to the amount of bond was set for January 30, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the Parties obtain more information to determine a correct amount of bond, they can come back in to Court.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-05-2012 Continued To Dec-19-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
